(3 comments)

I am having a bit of second thought at the broad brush we did here for the globs.

Should we have two globs, for the two types of accesses we really have?
I know it's kind of a pity now that these patches passed testing and all.

Perhaps as a follow on patch? Or is it totally bad for some reason I am not realizing?

Patch Set 7:

also see my comment in the next patch.
Perhaps we should just drop lnet/lustre from the end of the glob and make the caller provide it for clarity?

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 7:

If you really want to consider doing more full paths which consider creating  new  tool.

Patch Set 7:

Oleg, I don't think having a separate glob will change anything. If we have two files with the same name then there will be problems accessing it via lctl regardless, so that is just something we will need to avoid.
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 4aed5234f1123efc06c5c7e702085a461a8aae12

parents.subject: LU-7506 lfs: "lfs quota -h" should support petabytes output

commit.subject: LU-5030 libcfs: create cfs_get_paths() function
commit.message:
LU-5030 libcfs: create cfs_get_paths() function

With the move of the upstream client to sysfs the
lustre and lnet utilites no long should directly
access the proc file system. To avoid this a new
function is created to support both the traditional
procfs lustre/lnet parameters as well as the new
sysfs lustre/lnet parameters as well. Besides the
creation of these functions the LNet utilites are
converted to use these new functions.

Change-Id: I78788f41955073e1e48126103ef2aec13d64812b
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Signed-off-by: Wang Chao <chao.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17459
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Ryan Haasken <haasken@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Emoly Liu <emoly.liu@intel.com>
